title: Famet Enterprises

Featured Businesses


The Trucking School
A CDL training business focused on providing hands-on, compliant, and career-ready driver education, helping students gain the skills and confidence needed to enter the trucking industry.


CDL Dashboard
A web-based application deployed on Vercel that supports The Trucking School by streamlining student engagement and showcasing key information through a clean, user-focused interface.

title: The Trucking School
text:
Hands-on CDL training for real-world careers

 Visit Website

title: The Trucking School
text:
Business Overview
The Trucking School is a CDL training business dedicated to preparing students for real careers in the trucking industry through hands-on instruction and practical, job-ready education. The school focuses on helping individuals gain the skills, confidence, and knowledge needed to successfully enter a high-demand field with clear career pathways.

The Problem
The business addresses a common challenge in driver training: many programs focus on testing rather than real-world readiness. Students often complete programs without the practical experience needed to perform confidently on the job.


The Approach
The Trucking School emphasizes what matters most for career success:

Practical driving experience — real seat time behind the wheel, not just classroom theory
Safety-first instruction — building habits that protect drivers and the public
Structured curriculum — training that reflects actual industry expectations and standards
Regulatory compliance — ensuring students meet all CDL licensing requirements



Who It Serves

Career changers looking to enter a stable, high-demand industry
Entry-level drivers seeking their first CDL credential
Individuals pursuing long-term, reliable employment with clear growth pathways



Looking Ahead
The Trucking School is focused on sustainable growth through improved systems, operational efficiency, and strategic partnerships. The long-term goal is to scale the business while maintaining training quality, compliance, and a strong student experience.


Role: Founder & Operator | Industry: Transportation & Education | Website: the-trucking-school.com

title: CDL Scheduler
text: Instructor capacity scheduling system for The Trucking School

title: CDL Scheduler
text:
Live Product Demo

  Open App in New Tab 
  View Source on GitHub

title: CDL Scheduler
text:
Overview
This project involved using Claude Code to build a full internal scheduling tool for The Trucking School. The CDL Scheduler replaces manual coordination happening across spreadsheets, texts, and phone calls with a single operational dashboard.
The primary purpose of this app is to:

Give managers real-time visibility into instructor capacity across all shifts
Prevent instructor overload by enforcing student assignment limits
Replace a complex master spreadsheet with a simple, purpose-built tool


The Problem
The Trucking School was managing scheduling manually — across a master Google Sheet, CRM exports, texts, and phone calls. With 4 instructors, 4 daily shifts, and 20–60 active students, the process was fragile and time-consuming. There was no single place to see who was assigned where, which instructors had open capacity, or which students were still waiting to be scheduled.


The Solution
The CDL Scheduler addresses this with a purpose-built internal tool featuring:

Dashboard — Weekly shift view (Morning / Afternoon / Evening / Saturday) showing active students per shift
Instructor Capacity — Real-time load tracking with visual progress bars and shift badges
Student Assignment — Assign students to instructors with capacity enforcement built in
Edit & Reassign — Change shift, instructor, or dates for any student at any time
All Students Roster — Complete history of current and past students with status tracking
Instructors Page — Add, edit, and delete instructors with open spots and availability at a glance



Technical Details
Framework/Stack: Next.js 16, React 19, JavaScript, Tailwind CSS v4
Database: PostgreSQL via Supabase (instructors, students, assignments tables)
Deployment: Vercel (production) + GitHub (source control)
Design: The Trucking School brand colors (red #dc2626, jet black), bold typography, responsive grid layout
Tools Used:

Claude Code — AI-assisted development for full-stack build
Supabase — PostgreSQL database with Row Level Security
Vercel — Production deployment and hosting
GitHub — Version control and source code management

title: CDL Scheduler
text:
Product Spec
The CDL Scheduler was built from a structured MVP spec focused on one core action:

Assign a student to an instructor without exceeding instructor capacity.

Key decisions made during the build:

Shifts (Morning / Afternoon / Evening / Saturday) added after reviewing the existing master spreadsheet
Start and end dates on every assignment to support the weekly active view
One assignment per student enforced at the database level (unique constraint)
Capacity check runs server-side before every assignment to prevent overload
No auth in MVP — internal tool, RLS disabled for speed of iteration


Role: Founder & Developer | Stack: Next.js + Supabase + Tailwind | Live App: cdl-scheduler.vercel.app

title: Business Case Presentation
text: This project focused on developing a comprehensive business case and pitch presentation for a new product opportunity. The assignment required identifying a market need, analyzing the opportunity, and presenting a compelling case for why the product should be built and funded.

title: Business Case Presentation
text: This project focused on developing a comprehensive business case and pitch presentation for a new product opportunity. The assignment required identifying a market need, analyzing the opportunity, and presenting a compelling case for why the product should be built and funded.

title: Business Case Presentation
text:
Executive Summary
Product Concept: [Brief description of your proposed product/service]
Market Opportunity: [Size and growth potential of target market]
Investment Required: [Estimated funding needed]
Expected Return: [Projected revenue and profitability timeline]
Recommendation: [Clear go/no-go recommendation with supporting rationale]

title: Business Case Presentation
text:
Problem Statement

Market Problem

Issue Identification: What specific problem does your product solve?
Target Audience: Who experiences this problem most acutely?
Current Solutions: How are people solving this problem today?
Gap Analysis: What’s missing from existing solutions?



Quantifying the Problem

Market Size: How many people/organizations face this issue?
Economic Impact: What does this problem cost users currently?
Urgency: Why is now the right time to solve this problem?
Evidence: What data supports the existence and scale of this problem?

title: Business Case Presentation
text:
Market Analysis

Industry Overview

Market Size: Total Addressable Market (TAM), Serviceable Addressable Market (SAM)
Growth Trends: Historical growth rates and future projections
Key Drivers: What factors are driving market growth?
Market Maturity: Where is this market in its lifecycle?



Competitive Landscape

Direct Competitors: Who offers similar solutions?
Indirect Competitors: What alternative approaches exist?
Competitive Advantages: How will you differentiate?
Market Positioning: Where will you fit in the competitive landscape?



Customer Analysis

Target Segments: Primary and secondary customer groups
User Personas: Detailed profiles of ideal customers
Buying Behavior: How do customers make purchase decisions?
Price Sensitivity: What are customers willing to pay?

title: Business Case Presentation
text:
Solution Overview

Product Vision

Core Value Proposition: What unique value do you provide?
Key Features: What are the essential product capabilities?
User Experience: How will customers interact with your product?
Differentiation: What makes your solution superior?



Product Strategy

Development Approach: Build vs. buy vs. partner decisions
Technology Stack: What technical foundation will you use?
Scalability: How will the product grow with demand?
Innovation: What makes this solution innovative?

title: Business Case Presentation
text:
Business Model

Revenue Model

Pricing Strategy: How will you price your product?
Revenue Streams: What are your sources of income?
Customer Acquisition: How will you attract customers?
Unit Economics: What are your cost and revenue per customer?



Financial Projections

Revenue Forecast: 3-5 year revenue projections
Cost Structure: Development, operational, and marketing costs
Profitability Timeline: When will you reach break-even?
Investment Requirements: Funding needed for different milestones



Go-to-Market Strategy

Launch Strategy: How will you introduce the product?
Marketing Channels: How will you reach customers?
Sales Strategy: Direct, partner, or hybrid approach?
Customer Success: How will you ensure customer satisfaction?

title: Business Case Presentation
text:
Implementation Plan

Development Roadmap

Phase 1: MVP development and initial testing
Phase 2: Full product launch and market entry

Phase 3: Scale and expansion opportunities
Milestones: Key deliverables and timeline



Resource Requirements

Team Structure: What roles and skills are needed?
Technology Infrastructure: What systems and tools are required?
Capital Requirements: Funding needs by phase
Strategic Partnerships: What alliances will accelerate success?



Risk Analysis

Market Risks: What could change in the market?
Technical Risks: What development challenges might arise?
Competitive Risks: How might competitors respond?
Mitigation Strategies: How will you address each risk?

title: Business Case Presentation
text:
Success Metrics

Key Performance Indicators

Customer Metrics: Acquisition, retention, satisfaction
Business Metrics: Revenue, profitability, market share
Product Metrics: Usage, engagement, feature adoption
Operational Metrics: Efficiency, quality, scalability



Success Criteria

Short-term (6 months): [Specific measurable goals]
Medium-term (1-2 years): [Growth and market position targets]
Long-term (3-5 years): [Vision achievement and expansion goals]

title: Business Case Presentation
text:
Investment Recommendation

Case for Investment

Market Opportunity: Large, growing market with clear need
Competitive Advantage: Defensible differentiation and positioning
Team Capability: Right skills and experience to execute
Financial Returns: Attractive risk-adjusted return potential
Strategic Fit: Aligns with organizational goals and capabilities



Expected Outcomes

Revenue Impact: [Projected revenue contribution]
Market Position: [Expected market share and positioning]
Strategic Value: [Broader organizational benefits]
Growth Platform: [Future expansion opportunities]

title: Business Case Presentation
text:
Next Steps

Immediate Actions (30 days)

Secure initial funding/approval
Finalize team structure and hiring
Complete technical feasibility analysis
Develop detailed project plan



Short-term Milestones (90 days)

Complete market validation research
Build MVP and conduct user testing
Establish key partnerships
Refine go-to-market strategy



Long-term Goals (1 year)

Launch product to target market
Achieve customer acquisition targets
Demonstrate product-market fit
Plan expansion opportunities

title: Business Case Presentation
text:
Appendices

Supporting Data

Market research reports and analysis
Customer interview findings
Competitive analysis details
Financial model assumptions



Presentation Materials

Slide Deck: [Link to presentation slides]
Executive Summary: [One-page business case summary]

Financial Model: [Detailed financial projections]
Supporting Research: [Market data and analysis]


Skills Demonstrated: Business Strategy, Market Analysis, Financial Modeling, Presentation Design, Strategic Planning
Tools Used: PowerPoint/Keynote, Excel/Google Sheets, Market Research Databases, Survey Tools

title: CDL Dashboard
text:
Live Product Demo

   Open App in New Tab 
   View Source on GitHub

title: CDL Dashboard
text:
Overview
This project involved using Claude Code to develop a full student dashboard for The Trucking School. The CDL Dashboard provides students with a centralized portal to track their CDL training progress, view test scores, access certificates, and manage account settings.
The primary purpose of this app is to:

Give students real-time visibility into their training progress across CDL classes
Provide a clean, professional interface for viewing test scores and earned certificates
Streamline the student experience with a modern, responsive dashboard


The Problem
CDL training programs often lack a digital student experience. Students have limited visibility into their progress, scores, and certification status, relying on phone calls or in-person visits to get updates. This creates friction for both students and administrators.


The Solution
The CDL Dashboard addresses this gap with a purpose-built student portal featuring:

Dashboard Home — Welcome overview with key stats, training progress, and recent activity
Profile — Student information, enrollment details, and class history
Documents — Earned certificates with download access and in-progress tracking
Training — Module-by-module progress with completion status and scores
Testing — Complete score history with pass/fail indicators and averages
Settings — Notification preferences, appearance options, and account management



Technical Details
Framework/Stack: Next.js 14, React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS
Deployment: Vercel (production) + GitHub (source control)
Design: Dark theme with glassmorphism cards, red accent branding, responsive sidebar layout, staggered animations
Tools Used:

Claude Code — AI-assisted development for building components, pages, and data models
Vercel — Production deployment and hosting
GitHub — Version control and source code management

title: CDL Dashboard
text:
Product Requirements Document
The CDL Dashboard was built from a detailed implementation plan that specified:

6 dashboard pages with consistent navigation and design language
Mock data for a sample student (John Martinez) with Class A (complete, 87%) and Class B (in progress, 67%)
Reusable components including StatCard, ProgressBar, ScoreBadge, CertificateCard, and ActivityItem
Responsive design with a collapsible sidebar for mobile and a sticky header
Dark glassmorphism theme matching The Trucking School branding (slate-900 background, #e00000 red accent, Lexend Deca font)


Role: Founder & Developer | Stack: Next.js + Tailwind CSS | Live App: cdl-dashboard.vercel.app

title: About Me
text:
Background
I have a background that combines engineering problem-solving with business strategy, which drives my passion for product management. I enjoy understanding real user problems, turning ambiguity into clear decisions, and working across teams to build practical, scalable solutions. What motivates me most is creating products that deliver real value by aligning user needs, technical execution, and business impact.

Education

Degree Name, Manufacturing Engineering, BYU
Degree Name, MBA, BYU



Experience

Founder - The Trucking School
2023 to Present

Founded and scaled a CDL training business, overseeing day-to-day operations, curriculum development, instructor management, and regulatory compliance
Drove student enrollment and revenue growth through targeted digital marketing and strategic partnerships
Leveraged business development, operations management, CRM systems, and customer acquisition strategies to build efficient processes and deliver consistent training outcomes



Founder - CDL Classroom
2025 to Present

Founded CDL Classroom, an online CDL education platform, responsible for product development, content creation, and platform strategy
Built scalable digital learning resources to support student success
Applied skills in product planning, user-focused design, digital education tools, and process optimization to create an accessible and efficient learning experience




Skills & Interests
I bring a strong mix of technical and analytical skills, including Python, R, SQL, data visualization tools, and product management platforms, paired with clear communication, cross-functional collaboration, and strategic problem-solving. Outside of work, I’m driven by entrepreneurship, continuous learning, and building products that create real-world impact.


What’s Next
I’m focused on continuing to grow and scale my businesses by building better systems, improving the customer experience, and using data to guide smarter decisions. I’m especially interested in partnerships, product improvements, and tools that help streamline operations, expand reach, and create long-term, sustainable growth.
